items.summary: Foreign Minister Penny Wong has told a parliamentary hearing she believes the allegations of Australian activists, who say they were sexually assaulted while detained by Israeli forces.

items.title: Government takes PFAS environmental contamination case to court
items.summary: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

items.title: Advocates raise concerns over child safety inquiry's recommendations to elevate adoption in permanency hierarchy
items.summary: Queensland's Commission of Inquiry into Child Safety report, released by the state government on Wednesday, found the availability of adoption should "not be limited by cultural background or ethnicity".

items.title: Bondi Beach terror attack hero Ahmed Al Ahmed charged with assault
items.summary: Ahmed Al Ahmed has been charged with domestic violence common assault after an incident in Bankstown in March but told the ABC "it's not true at all".

items.title: WA 'spud king' Tony Galati's business fined $20k for unlicensed chip factory
items.summary: Potato baron Tony Galati's business group has been fined $20,000 after it was found to have built an illegal Spuddies potato chip factory in WA's South West.

items.title: Coach defends doctors as Crows players play out game after head clash
items.summary: Matthew Nicks defends his medical staff after two Crows were cleared to play out their win against the Cats without a concussion test after a nasty head clash.

items.title: Ex-WA teacher Kurt Charlton jailed for sexually abusing children he was babysitting
items.summary: Kurt David Charlton, a former WA teacher, is sentenced for sexual offences against nine victims, who were all aged under 13 at the time.

items.title: Thursday finance with David Chau
items.summary: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

items.title: Souths stung by 'sticky spray' ruling as Manly continue Foran resurgence
items.summary: The Manly Sea Eagles have provisionally moved into the NRL top four with a 28-14 win over the South Sydney Rabbitohs at Brookvale Oval.

items.title: Australian woman faces jail time over alleged cannabis vape liquid in Indonesia
items.summary: The woman reportedly told officers she used the cannabis liquid to treat knee pain and depression, but authorities say she faces up to 20 years in jail and a hefty fine.

items.title: RBA not concerned about stagflation or wage-price spiral
items.summary: RBA governor Michele Bullock says she is not concerned about stagflation or a wage-price spiral

items.title: Former Married at First Sight Australia bride Tahnee Cook says she felt pressure to be intimate on the show
items.summary: Married At First Sight UK has become the centre of rape allegations. Now former MAFS Australia contestants and producers are speaking out about being coerced into intimacy.

items.title: 'Did he just swear?': Senate estimates rolls on, government under pressure
items.summary: This week the Albanese government was under pressure on multiple fronts, Senate Estimates got a little sweary and Tony Abbott said he hadn't taken a "vow of silence".

items.title: Full Circle launches Adelaide musical theatre company with Catch Me If You Can
items.summary: Musical theatre typically draws strong audiences in Adelaide where a troupe dedicated to the genre has just launched a professional company that will produce Catch Me If You Can in November.

items.title: As Beijing cracks down on VPNs, internet users in China find ways around the Great Firewall
items.summary: People in China say it is increasingly difficult to access the open internet, but they continue to find ways around Beijing's censorship apparatus.

items.title: Canberra Liberals say alleged violent threat made against former leader Leanne Castley was 'misunderstanding'
items.summary: The Canberra Liberals have distanced themselves from former leader Leanne Castley's allegation that she was threatened with physical assault, saying the remark related to an "animated discussion" that was quickly resolved.

items.title: EPA pushes back on need for real-time data on antibiotic use in Tasmania's salmon pens
items.summary: The director of Tasmania's Environment Protection Authority rejects calls to release real-time information on the amount of antibiotics used by salmon producers, despite the companies already providing that information to other government bodies.

items.title: Sacked Sydney nurses argue video showing alleged antisemitic threats was recorded illegally
items.summary: A lawyer acting for one of two Sydney nurses accused of making antisemitic threats via an online video chat app has told a court their actions were "stupid things said by stupid people".

items.title: SA Budget 2026: Here are the winners and losers

items.title: Big spending on health and housing in 'no surprises' SA budget
items.summary: South Australia's debt is projected to hit to more than $50 billion by 2029-30, with health taking up about one third of the budget.

items.title: Queensland minister Tim Nicholls 'acknowledges trauma' of Wolston Park survivors but does not apologise
items.summary: Victims of alleged abuse at a Queensland mental health facility have been left disappointed after the state's health minister did not offer a public apology.

items.title: Greens question whether Jim Chalmers could exempt home owners in his electorate from CGT changes
items.summary: While the party has indicated it is willing to help Labor pass the tax laws — which includes changes to negative gearing, capital gains tax (CGT) discount, trusts and the $250 tax offset — by early July, the Greens hold concerns about specifics in the bill.

items.title: Victorian vehicle theft rates rise to buck trend across mainland Australia

items.title: Socceroos emerging star Lucas Herrington cruising into maiden World Cup

items.title: Aged Care Minister Sam Rae grilled over human involvement in aged care assessments

items.title: Four New Zealand MPs banned from China, Hong Kong and Macau after Taiwan trip

items.title: Pat Cummins hopeful of playing every Test of Australia's hectic 2026/27 schedule

items.title: Trump's tariff game has resumed and Australia could face some curveballs
items.summary: Donald Trump had been using tariffs to increase his power over other countries until America's Supreme Court stepped in. He was never going to cede that power easily.

items.title: Thomas Sewell was training at a Melbourne boxing gym. Now it's facing the consequences
items.summary: ABC NEWS Verify can reveal that neo-Nazi Thomas Sewell has been training at a boxing gym in the outer-Melbourne suburb of Diamond Creek, a short drive from where he is living. The gym has been suspended by the sport's governing body.

items.title: Crop failure fears as deadly bee parasite wipes out hives and forces beekeepers to quit

items.title: PM says Australia has 'ideological disagreement' with Trump administration after US reveals anti-slavery tariff
items.summary: The planned new tariff is to be slapped on dozens of countries that have allegedly failed to take action to prevent slavery and forced labour.

items.title: EPA asked to pause Karara magnetite mine assessment over water concerns
items.summary: As a major miner seeks approval to extend its magnetite ore operations in regional Western Australia to 2048, shires are increasingly concerned about ensuring there's enough groundwater to go around.

items.summary: Militant attacks have hampered efforts to combat the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda.

items.title: Craig Foster concerned that FIFA World Cup players are not speaking out on US human rights record

items.title: Why TV renovation shows are a 'huge gamble' for their creators
items.summary: A planned 12-month build that's still underway three years later is the running joke of design shows. But what happens when you're a TV producer trying to make a series of it?

items.summary: An independent expert has urged Australia to "make sure that things are done properly", while Woodside pushed back against her involvement.

items.title: Matteo Berrettini withdraws injured at French Open as Matteo Arnaldi progresses to face Flavio Cobolli
items.summary: Matteo Berrettini is once again forced out of a match by injury, retiring in the second set of his French Open quarterfinal with compatriot Matteo Arnaldi.

items.title: More than 800 competitors set to take on Finke Desert Race in 50th anniversary year
items.summary: The 460-kilometre test of skill and endurance through the central Australian outback has become one of the most famous off-road races in the world.

items.title: Second Chance Choir helping people overcome hardship through music

items.title: Kakadu's Jim Jim and Twin Falls to stay closed throughout NT's peak tourism season

items.title: Aryna Sabalenka 'just wants to quit tennis' after French Open quarterfinal loss to Diana Shnaider
items.summary: Aryna Sabalenka feels the pain of a shock loss in the quarterfinals of the French Open, saying she just feels like smashing things.

items.title: First Nations voters sent to back of queue for Voice election
items.summary: South Australia's Electoral Commission was aware that forcing First Nations voters to queue twice for the Voice election would be perceived as "discriminatory behaviour". Despite that, voters say it still happened and, in some cases, they were turned away.

items.title: Extreme heatwaves have become a major threat to Australia's food supply, and not just in summer
items.summary: Record-breaking heatwaves decimated crops in Australia this year and served as a "real wake-up call" for the urgent need to develop more heat-resistant crops and research into food security.

items.title: Charges laid over death and alleged kidnapping of man whose body was found near Melbourne playground
items.summary: Police raided several properties yesterday after the body of a man was found near a children's playground in Tarneit last month.

items.title: As Victor Wembanyama makes his NBA Finals debut, the league's new face is on its biggest stage
items.summary: After years of searching, the NBA finally has its new face in Victor Wembanyama, and he is about to grace the league's biggest stage.

items.title: Puppy farm anger sees breeder withdraw proposal for facility with mandatory caesareans
items.summary: An application for a proposed puppy farm in central Victoria has been withdrawn after lobbying by nearby residents and animal activists.

items.title: Australia chasing men's rugby sevens World Championship at final tournament in Bordeaux
items.summary: An unbeaten tournament victory in Spain gives Australia a chance of claiming the men's World Championship, with the title to be decided in this weekend's final round.

items.title: Queensland beekeepers paying thousands each month to fend off varroa mites as colonies devastated
items.summary: Beekeepers in Queensland say they are "barely hanging on" a year after the varroa mite was detected in the state for the first time.

items.title: NSW government to introduce medicinal cannabis driving reforms
items.summary: The Labor government plans to introduce a legal limit on the concentration of THC a driver can have in their system if they have a prescription.

items.title: Tasmania reconsiders bridge ban as Nuyina's refuelling saga drags on
items.summary: Authorities are considering whether it is feasible for Australia's Antarctic icebreaker to safely travel under Hobart's Tasman Bridge, as the Tasmanian government tries to resolve a costly refuelling saga.

items.title: Australia's economic slowdown is just beginning, economists warn, as recession risks rise
items.summary: The economy grew just 0.3 per cent in the first quarter, gross domestic product per capita went backwards, and interest rate rises and cost-of-living pressures are weighing on households.

items.title: Who could replace Hobart's outgoing lord mayor Anna Reynolds?
items.summary: After eight years in the top role, Anna Reynolds has announced she will not seek re-election in October. Her departure coincides with a notable reduction in the number of council representatives.

items.title: What do the pope and most Australians have in common? Neither trust AI companies
items.summary: Most Australians appear to distrust and dislike artificial intelligence despite using technology more than people in other countries.

items.title: Australia gains access to powerful AI amid questions about readiness
items.summary: Fresh access to AI deemed too dangerous for public release has ramped up pressure on the government to respond to possible risks.

items.title: Dive industry calls for more no-fish, no-drill zones as marine park review looms

items.title: Sportsbet owner ventures into giveaways with rewards club, anti-gambling advocates concerned
items.summary: One of the world's biggest gambling companies is breaking into the 'rewards club' scene, raising the alarm among anti-gambling advocates.

items.title: Falling house prices bring relief for some while a financial nightmare for others
items.summary: A 46-year-old former "forever renter" believes her house is now worth less than what she paid for it and she is at risk of slipping into negative equity, which would mean her Marrickville home could be worth less than what she owes the bank.

items.summary: Patients have been left frustrated and scared after learning that a life-saving breast cancer and endometriosis drug is to be pulled from shelves in Australia and some other countries.

items.summary: Experts say healing might involve reducing emotional distress and gaining clarity from the situation.

items.title: After scooping the Oscars for Anora, Sean Baker brings his filmmaking tips to Sydney's Vivid Festival
